sessions.details: recorded 1967-11-27 for Top Gear. First TX 1967-12-03
recorded 1980-04-14 for Friday Rock Show. First TX 1980-05-23
Wilson "Tonight's program was dedicated to Ian Morgan who wrote in to say I talk too much!"

track_listing.track: Hey Hey My My Into The Black

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Hello there. This is TV on the Radio. Thomas Vance. Good evening. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening, at the request of many, we re-broadcast two sets by The Pretty Things that were recorded in the 1960s.
And we repeat our set by the band called More, who we've just heard have been signed to a major American record company.
We've got three tracks from Drama, the new Yes LP, and other new cuts by Blue Oyster Cult and Black Axe,
and some classic cuts from The Floyd, Rainbow, Roxy Music, and Neil Young, among others.
Not forgetting our Friday Night Connection.
A little bit of titillation falls into the bath with you.
But this evening we start with Yes.
The Pink Floyd from Wish You Were Here and Shine On You, Crazy Diamond.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
And we've played The Floyd for loads of people, of course, just about everybody I would think who listens to this program,
including justin and adrian and michael good friends of simon wilson who lives in deal in
kent and also for animal and gonzo and totem angel lots of best wishes from wookiee who lives
in barton in torquay it seems you're going to be breaking up i hope wherever you go you all have a
good time and also for nicky and chris who's in hospital get well soon because i miss you it says
here and for sally and for paul congratulations on your engagement and for sarah and for steve
and all the lads in the world maybe and for trisha and caroline the twins and julie
And Rosemary, I think that is
And most of all, for Carolyn the Cucumber
From Astley Barlow
And for Carolyn, Dawn and Catherine
And Paul and Sarah and anybody else who went to see Pink Floyd
At Earl's Court last week
From Steve and also from Neil
And for Sean Tracy and Richard Watts and Adam and Ben and Paul Lunnan
And Paul Waters and Damien and Dave
And also for all the friends of Andrew Parr
Of the third form from Cowes High School
And the track that we played before that
Was something from the brand new album by
yes the album's called drama and the track was does it really happen 10 22 this is the friday
rock show and now the first of four from our repeat session by the band called more
an interesting album the album's called cultosaurus erectus and it features the talent
[Music]
[Music]
of blue oyster cult the track you heard there was called black blade especially there for vernon and
wendy and steve and karen who were down there in worthing where i was last night as indeed was
Gary Edwards, he's from Shoreham, and just says here, love all.
And also Pete and Tony and everybody at the Hungry Years in Brighton wanted to mention.
Prior to that you heard Atomic Masters, the first of four that we're going to have from Moore tonight,
a session that we first put out some time ago,
and it's really nice to hear that they have signed with a major company in America,
a company by the name of Atlantic. I'm sure they'll do remarkably well.
It's 28 minutes now before 11 o'clock, and here's the first of two sets we have dug out of the archives for you,
recorded in the mid-60s by a band called The Pretty Things.
And that certainly is one of the oldest archive sessions
[Music]
I think we've ever featured on The Friday Rock Show.
We believe it was recorded in October in 1964.
It was the band called The Pretty Things
that were formed by the original bass guitarist of the Rolling Stones,
a man by the name of Dick Taylor, at Sidcup Art College in 1963.
And also in the line-up were Phil May,
who's just reformed the band, incidentally,
a new album's coming out next week,
and the mad drummer himself, Viv Prince.
and the three tracks that he heard were
Hey Mama, Don't Bring Me Down and Big Boss Man
and incidentally Big Boss Man and Don't Bring Me Down
were singles for them and sort of minor hits.
Here's a good new band called Black Axe.
[Music]
Saxon, as if you didn't know from the album Wheels of Steel
and See the Light Shining.
That was for loads of people, including Simon Harwood
and his brothers Gary and Neil
who live in Pontyclun in mid-Glamorganshire
and also everybody who goes to the, I think it's Gillsborough School
lots of best wishes from Andy Hodges
who lives in Creighton and North Anse
and an unsigned gentleman
or maybe lady who lives in number two
Cameron Court Heathall in Dumfries wants me to say
hello to Maxie and Sar and Lanx and Stew
and Ewan and many many girls
at Dumfries High School third year
and I got a little
card here from St Helier in Jersey
and I've got to say hello to all the new
lower sixth at Hollier School
good luck with all their examinations for next year
and best wishes from Steve Thompson
And before that, you heard a single which is out on the metal label
from a band, a five-piece band, in fact, who come from Carlisle.
They're called Black Axe, and their single was Red Lights.
It is 11 minutes now before 11 o'clock.
I've got a card here from Chris and from Steve and Mark,
and it was sent from Wakefield, West Yorkshire,
and I'll read it out for you.
Dear Tom, please play something by Rainbow on Friday the 15th
as we will be camped under the stars at Castle Donington,
listening to the show and waiting for the festival on Saturday.
Please say hello to all the Normanton headbangers.
I tell you what, I'll say hello to everybody
who's camped under the stars
or maybe even travelling down to Castle Donington
for the Monsters of Rock Festival tomorrow.
I'm sure you're going to have a great, great day.
...solid band, that's more.
[Music]
[Music]
And one of our session tracks from then called I Have No Answers.
And before that, especially for everybody up there at Castle Donington,
Blackmore's Rainbow from Rainbow Rising
and the track we played for you was Stargazer.
Hope it's all right under the stars tonight.
And there's a lovely little PS, actually,
on this card from Steve and Chris and Mark
who are travelling down,
or rather camping out there at the moment,
to see the Monsters of Rock Festival.
It says, PS, does Tony Wilson actually do anything
during the show,
or is he just waiting for his big moment?
What?
Do you actually do anything during the show,
or are you just waiting for the big moment?
Let me have a look at this.
It's true.
Goodness, well, I'd say it's lucky for them
they haven't put their address down,
or I think I'd have been round with the boys.
Oh, the boys.
Actually, he doesn't do anything.
Thank you.
Thank you.
But next weekend, you've got a heck of a lot to do, Wilson.
Get back on that microphone,
because you're not going to be in the studio,
and for three days you're going to be at Reading.
And tell us what you're going to be doing.
That's a bit it.
Well, we're going to be recording
approximately a dozen other bands at Reading.
Gillen, UFO, Pat Travers, Whitesnake,
Def Leppard, all sorts.
Right, and they will be broadcast in when?
In the following five or six weeks in the Rock Show.
Pretty much like we did last year.
That's right.
As blocks of music.
Uh-huh.
Okay.
So he's got three nights and three days at Reading next weekend.
What do you do in your spare time?
Because you never seem to have any.
I have no answer for that.
Right.
It's time for the Friday Night Connection.
Now, last week, we played for you Delta Lady by Leon Russell from the album Best of Leon.
Alpha Centauri by UFO from the LP No Place to Run.
And Fight to the Finish by Gamma from the album of the same name.
The connection was delta, alpha, and gamma.
Three letters out of the Greek alphabet.
And just about everybody got it right, but the card that we pulled out is a rather large one.
It's from Chris Bocca Edwards of [redacted address], West Bromwich in the West Midlands.
Chris, you get yourself a £10 record token.
And also, this gentleman has put together a Friday Night Connection.
Three bits of music, and it's in there somewhere.
Friday Night Connection for this week, put together by Chris Edwards, [redacted address], West Brom, West Midlands.
Know what the connection is?
Well, I'm sure somebody out there has already got an inkling of what it's all about.
If you have, well, you could get yourself a £10 record token
by putting the answer down on a postcard, communication of any form, actually,
and sending it to the Friday Night Connection,
the Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
There's a £10 record token in the offing
And our best of luck
Let's have some more
Let's have some more
And here is some more
Class Band
[Music]
That was recorded in 1973 from the album For Your Pleasure
It was called Do The Strand
Requested by Mariel of [redacted address] in Nottage
For Plum and for Matt and for Vic up there in Aberdeen
With lots and lots of love to Plum
Also for Simon Skerritt from Waltham Abbey, Upshur in Essex
And also for David Reynolds
Also from Upshur in Waltham Abbey in Essex
And for Diane and Mick
A couple of very nice people
Who work at a great club in Worthing
And Diane asked me to tell you, Mick
That she loves you
Prior to that you heard a track by Moore
From our session from them
The track was called Soldier
And it's going to be on an album
Which I think I mentioned last week
Called Metal Explosion
Which is an album built around
The Friday Rock Show
And the various excellent sessions
That have been put together by Tony Wilson
That album will be in your shop soon
It's 26 minutes after 11 of the clock
here on the friday rock show many thanks for all your letters with regard to yes i played that track
into the lens last week from their new album drama generally speaking most of the letters
had an extremely positive attitude toward the new lineup of yes the sort of reservation that
existed in some of the letters was the fact that people are going to miss john anderson well i'm
going to miss john anderson just for a while because he's bound to be recording again soon
but i must say i'm still glad that there is a yes here's two tracks from drama first run through
the light and then tempus fugit like the majority of pieces of extremely complex music it takes a
[Music]
[Music]
little time to get acquainted with it and i'm certainly acquainted well with that because
that's about the fourth time i've heard that track and i think it's a monster piece of magic
a couple of tracks from the album called drama that's hitting the streets shortly
it's by yes tempus fugit was preceded by run through the light and now here's something by
by the Pretty Things from the 60s.
Real 60s nostalgia there, and psychedelia.
Three tracks from the Pretty Things that we believe were recorded between 1966 and early 1968.
The last one was Defecting Grey, and before that you heard Turning My Head,
and prior to that, Buzz the Jerk.
And Phil May has reformed the Pretty Things.
I don't actually know the lineup of the band, I know he's in it.
There's a new LP, it just came into the office a couple of days ago.
If you haven't heard it, we'll listen to it and probably play some of it on the show.
we dug the pretty things out of the archives
particularly for Carolyn Stacey who lives in Bournemouth
and also for Roy Walker who lives in Whitstable
and Nigel Edwards who lives in Halliwell
that's up by Bolton
and Mark Rawston who's in Bury-in-Lanks
and everybody else who happens to like the pretty things
and now back to more
Pat Travers Band and Snorting Whiskey
[Music]
[Music]
For all the mates of Nick Hodgson, of Bradford,
all the hundreds that he's put in this letter,
good evening to you.
On Rock on Saturday tomorrow, we've got that madman, Ted Nugent,
also the Tigers of Pantang, Girl School,
and Wishbone Ash for 60 minutes in concert
between 4 and 7.30, you can hear that lot.
Tony Wilson is now going to speak.
Speak, Mr Wilson.
Tonight's programme is dedicated to Ian Morgan,
who wrote in to say that I talk too much.
Which is absolutely true, of course.
Thank you, Mr Morgan.
Thank you, Mr Wilson.
Oh, Grant Way, happy birthday to you from Angie.
That's Angie who lives in Ware in Harts.
Ian was our engineer tonight. Thank you, Ian.
And this is Tommy Vance in London, wherever you happen to be.
I hope you're well and I hope you're happy.
And if you're there for the monsters of rock at Castle Donington, have a good one.
God bless and good night.
It's midnight and we're together on all wavelengths, 433, 330, 285, 275 and VHF.
And this is Patrick Lunt in the Midnight Newsroom.
The headlines, there are fears that Gene Waddell, the...
